# Documentation Index

An index for discovering relevant documents during Plan and Explore execution

## Usage

Identify and read task-related sections below:

- Modify `src/` or `tests/` → Read **Python Development** and **Specifications** sections
- Modify `docs/specs/` → Read **Python Development** section and the target **Source Code** section
- Modify `docs/design/` → Read **Python Development** and **Design Guide** sections

## Python Development

- [Architecture design](docs/design/architecture.md): Python application architecture based on Onion Architecture, Composition Root + Orchestrator pattern, Protocol-based DI, type design, error handling, and logging - Use when modifying `src/` or `tests/`, or when understanding component relationships, adding new features, evaluating design decisions, or implementing new packages
- [Development workflow](docs/design/workflow.md): Tech stack, development commands, key principles (TDD, 100% coverage, type hints, test isolation), and step-by-step development flow (pre-implementation → TDD cycle → post-implementation) - Use when starting implementation tasks, understanding development rules, or reviewing the standard workflow

## Specifications

### Feature

#### Transform feature

- [Transform requirement](docs/specs/transform/requirements.md): Requirements for the transform package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/transform/` or understanding text file reading, line numbering, datetime header, and result statistics
- [Transform design](docs/specs/transform/design.md): Design for the transform package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/transform/` or understanding component structure, processing flow, design decisions (thin wrappers, SrcText/DstText, Fake-based testing), and design rationale

#### Check feature

- [Check requirement](docs/specs/check/requirements.md): Requirements for the check package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/check/` or understanding the static analysis pipeline (file enumeration, AST parsing, rule application, violation filtering, report output)
- [Check design](docs/specs/check/design.md): Design for the check package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/check/` or understanding the pipeline pattern (collect → parse → run → filter → format), component structure, ignore/override/rule-filter mechanisms, and design rationale

#### List feature

- [List requirement](docs/specs/list/requirements.md): Requirements for the list module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/list/` or understanding rule listing in text/JSON format
- [List design](docs/specs/list/design.md): Design for the list module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/list/` or understanding ListOrchestrator, ListFormatterFactory, RuleSet integration, and the Orchestrator-Provider-Context pattern

#### View feature

- [View requirement](docs/specs/view/requirements.md): Requirements for the view module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/view/` or understanding rule detail display by RULE_ID in text/JSON format
- [View design](docs/specs/view/design.md): Design for the view module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/view/` or understanding ViewOrchestrator, ViewFormatterFactory, RuleSet lookup, and the Composition Root pattern

### Application

- [CLI requirement](docs/specs/cli/requirements.md): Requirements for the cli module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/cli.py` or understanding subcommand execution, global/subcommand-specific options, config priority, error handling, and exit codes
- [CLI design](docs/specs/cli/design.md): Design for the cli module - Use when modifying `src/paladin/cli.py`, adding new subcommands, adding CLI options, or understanding component structure (app/main_callback/main), two-phase flow (callback→command), design decisions (no business logic, AppConfig in ctx.obj, top-level exception handling), guardrails, and design rationale
- [Config requirement](docs/specs/config/requirements.md): Requirements for the config package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/config/` or understanding environment variable loading, path construction, and runtime config composition
- [Config design](docs/specs/config/design.md): Design for the config package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/config/`, adding new config fields, or understanding component structure (EnvVarConfig / PathConfig / AppConfig), processing flow, design decisions (3-layer separation, pydantic-settings validation, PathConfig as internal component), and design rationale
- [Protocol requirement](docs/specs/protocol/requirements.md): Requirements for the protocol package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/protocol/` or understanding shared Port definitions in Onion Architecture
- [Protocol design](docs/specs/protocol/design.md): Design for the protocol package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/protocol/` or understanding TextFileSystemReaderProtocol/TextFileSystemWriterProtocol, read-write separation pattern, and structural subtyping

### Foundation

- [Error requirement](docs/specs/foundation/error/requirements.md): Requirements for the error package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/error/` or understanding application-wide error handling standardization
- [Error design](docs/specs/foundation/error/design.md): Design for the error package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/error/` or understanding ApplicationError, ErrorHandler, and centralized exception/logging strategy
- [Filesystem requirement](docs/specs/foundation/fs/requirements.md): Requirements for the fs package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/fs/` or understanding Protocol-based file I/O abstraction and side-effect isolation
- [Filesystem design](docs/specs/foundation/fs/design.md): Design for the fs package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/fs/` or understanding TextFileSystemReader, Port/Adapter pattern, and Fake-based test isolation
- [Logging requirement](docs/specs/foundation/log/requirements.md): Requirements for the log package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/log/` or understanding centralized logging configuration and function tracing
- [Logging design](docs/specs/foundation/log/design.md): Design for the log package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/log/` or understanding `@log` decorator, LogConfigurator, and cross-cutting concern implementation
- [Base model requirement](docs/specs/foundation/model/requirements.md): Requirements for the model package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/model/` or understanding shared domain model constraints
- [Base model design](docs/specs/foundation/model/design.md): Design for the model package - Use when modifying `src/paladin/foundation/model/` or understanding CoreModel, boundary validation pattern, and immutability/strict-validation defaults

## Source Code

### Business Logic

- [Transform feature](src/paladin/transform/): Reference feature package with Context, Orchestrator, OrchestratorProvider (Composition Root), Reader, Transformer, Writer, and Result types - Use when modifying `docs/specs/transform/`, adding new feature packages, understanding the Orchestrator-Provider-Context pattern, or reviewing the canonical package structure
- [rule package](src/paladin/rule/): Rule domain package containing Rule Protocol, RuleSet (rule management and execution), RuleSetFactory (production rule set construction), SourceFile/SourceFiles types (representing Python source files as inspection targets), Violation/Violations/RuleMeta types, and all concrete rule implementations - Use when adding new rules, modifying rule logic, understanding the rule domain architecture, or working with source file representations
- [check package](src/paladin/check/): Static analysis core with CheckOrchestrator, CheckOrchestratorProvider, CheckContext, FileCollector, AstParser, ViolationFilter, RuleFilter, PathExcluder, OverrideResolver, and CheckFormatterFactory - Use when modifying `docs/specs/check/`, understanding the analysis pipeline, or working with ignore/override/formatting logic
- [list package](src/paladin/list/): Rule listing with ListOrchestrator, ListOrchestratorProvider, ListContext, and ListFormatterFactory - Use when modifying `docs/specs/list/` or understanding the `paladin list` command's business logic
- [view package](src/paladin/view/): Rule detail display with ViewOrchestrator, ViewOrchestratorProvider, ViewContext, and ViewFormatterFactory - Use when modifying `docs/specs/view/` or understanding the `paladin view` command's business logic
- [version package](src/paladin/version/): Version string resolution with VersionOrchestrator, VersionOrchestratorProvider, and VersionResolver - Use when modifying the `paladin version` command's business logic

### CLI

- [CLI entry point](src/paladin/cli.py): Typer-based CLI that assembles Config, Context, and OrchestratorProvider then delegates to Orchestrator - Use when modifying `docs/specs/cli/`, adding new commands, understanding the application startup flow, or reviewing how Config/Context/Provider are wired together

### Config

- [config package](src/paladin/config/): Configuration management with AppConfig, EnvVarConfig, PathConfig (runtime config composition), ProjectConfig, ProjectConfigLoader (pyproject.toml parsing), TargetResolver, and OverrideEntry - Use when modifying `docs/specs/config/`, adding new config fields, understanding environment variable loading, path construction, or project-level settings (rules, include/exclude, per-file-ignores, overrides)

### Protocol

- [protocol package](src/paladin/protocol/): Shared Port definitions with TextFileSystemReaderProtocol and TextFileSystemWriterProtocol - Use when modifying `docs/specs/protocol/`, adding new shared Protocols, or understanding Onion Architecture Port/Adapter boundaries

### Foundation

- [Filesystem abstraction](src/paladin/foundation/fs/): Protocol-based file I/O with TextFileSystemReader - Use when modifying `docs/specs/foundation/fs/`, adding new I/O operations, implementing Protocol adapters, or understanding the Port/Adapter pattern
- [Error handling](src/paladin/foundation/error/): ApplicationError base class and ErrorHandler - Use when modifying `docs/specs/foundation/error/`, implementing custom errors, understanding exception flow, or modifying error logging
- [Logging](src/paladin/foundation/log/): `@log` decorator (auto-records args and return values) and LogConfigurator - Use when modifying `docs/specs/foundation/log/`, adding logging to new components, understanding cross-cutting concern implementation, or configuring log output
- [Base model](src/paladin/foundation/model/): CoreModel for external data mapping - Use when modifying `docs/specs/foundation/model/`, adding new data models for JSON/YAML input or understanding the boundary validation pattern

## Coding Standards

- [pyproject.toml design](docs/design/pyproject.md): Configuration rationale for build system, linter, and type checker - Use when modifying or reviewing `pyproject.toml` settings, understanding ruff/pyright rule choices, or reviewing tool configuration decisions
- [Specification design](docs/design/specs.md): Purpose and design philosophy of specs/ documents for AI-assisted development, authoring guidelines, and operational workflow - Use when modifying or reviewing `docs/specs/` documents, understanding why `docs/specs/` exists, or reviewing synchronization requirements
- [Ignore interface design](docs/design/ignore.md): Ignore feature interface design — inline comment syntax (`# paladin: ignore`), file-level ignore, `per-file-ignores` in `pyproject.toml`, and `--ignore-rule` CLI option - Use when implementing R-080 to R-083, designing the ignore feature, or understanding the config file strategy
- [Configuration file interface design](docs/design/configuration.md): Configuration file interface design — rule enable/disable (`[tool.paladin.rules]`), include/exclude path control, rule-specific settings (`[tool.paladin.rule."<id>"]`), and directory-specific overrides (`[[tool.paladin.overrides]]`) - Use when implementing R-090 to R-093, designing the configuration file feature, or understanding the full `[tool.paladin]` section structure

## Introduction

- [Design concept](docs/intro/concept.md): Tool positioning, background, target values, optimization priorities, diagnostic philosophy, and scope strategy - Use when understanding why Paladin exists, what it optimizes for, or how it relates to existing tools
- [Specifications](docs/intro/specifications.md): Target scope, functional requirements, diagnostic output requirements, quality requirements, rule requirements, and constraints - Use when understanding what Paladin must satisfy or reviewing acceptance criteria
- [CLI interface draft](docs/intro/interface.md): Command structure (check/list/view/version), check output contract, exit codes, JSON/text output format - Use when understanding the CLI design direction or reviewing command options
- [Repository structure](docs/intro/structure.md): Complete file layout with descriptions of each file and directory's role - Use when locating files, understanding project organization, or verifying the actual directory tree
- [Backlog](docs/intro/backlog.md): List of features mentioned in other intro documents but not yet implemented - Use when reviewing unimplemented CLI options or planning future features

---

This file is inspired by [llms.txt](https://llmstxt.org/). Rather than being published on the web, it serves as an index for AI to understand the repository context.
